title: sphenandra viscosa
intro: Sphenandra viscosa, also known as sticky vine, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its sticky, milky sap and long, woody stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Sphenandra viscosa include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy in Australian indigenous medicine. Herbal preparations of Sphenandra viscosa typically involve infusing the leaves in hot water or mixing the sap with other herbs to create a treatment for various health conditions.
